revenue_by_merchant.sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t, dim_merchant m
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue
LIMIT 10;

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query calculates total revenue without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Potential performance issue
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially less performant queries.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each clause.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and a readability issue with implicit join syntax. It also lacked comments.

customer_spend.sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

issues.title: Incorrect SUM calculation
issues.problem: The SUM calculation includes all transactions, not just completed ones.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The filter should be applied in the JOIN condition to ensure only completed transactions are considered.
issues.fix: LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id AND t.status = 'COMPLETED'

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and calculations.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The aliases could be more consistent (e.g., total_lifetime_value, total_completed_orders).

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_completed_orders FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query has correctness issues with the SUM calculation and missing filter in the JOIN condition. Readability can be improved with comments and consistent naming.

daily_failure_rate.sql:
SELECT transaction_date,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2
        WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED'
        AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       failed_count / total_count * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
GROUP BY transaction_date
ORDER BY transaction_date;

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause, it's defined in a subquery.
issues.fix: Use a subquery or a CTE to calculate the failure rate correctly.

issues.title: Potential performance issue with correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN or a CTE to pre-aggregate failed counts by transaction_date.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH daily_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS total_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  GROUP BY transaction_date
),
failure_rates AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, failed_count, total_count, (failed_count * 100.0 / total_count) AS failure_rate
  FROM daily_transactions
)
SELECT transaction_date, failed_count, total_count, failure_rate
FROM failure_rates
ORDER BY transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the calculation of the failure rate, a performance issue with the correlated subquery, and a readability issue due to lack of comments.
